output_text:
# Deep Research Report: Modafinil Cognitive-Enhancement Literature

## Executive Summary

Modafinil shows a consistent but modest effect on sustained attention and
wakefulness in sleep-deprived populations, with weaker and more heterogeneous
evidence for executive-function gains in rested, healthy adults. The strongest
randomized trials concentrate in shift-work and narcolepsy cohorts; off-label
cognitive-enhancement claims rest on smaller, lower-powered studies.

## Findings

- Sustained attention and vigilance improve reliably under sleep deprivation
  (medium effect sizes across replicated trials).
- Working-memory and planning effects in rested adults are small and
  inconsistent, with several null results.
- Tolerability is favorable at standard doses; headache and insomnia are the
  most frequently reported adverse events.

## Risks

- Publication bias toward positive cognitive-enhancement results in small
  samples.
- Heterogeneous outcome batteries make cross-study synthesis fragile.

## Sources

See citations below; all retrieved from open bibliographic indices.

citations.title: Cognitive effects of modafinil in healthy individuals: a systematic review
Modafinil for shift-work sleep disorder: randomized controlled trial
Heterogeneity in executive-function outcomes with modafinil

request.query: Survey the cognitive-enhancement evidence for modafinil in healthy and sleep-deprived adults
